summary: The CEO reported two turns during this Programme's early work where the H-P01 prompt-optimization gate's required confirmation flow appeared not to run. A dedicated harness-engineering forensic pass (Kwame Asante) scanned the session's complete raw transcript and found exactly six genuine H-P01 firings in total, every one followed by the required AskUserQuestion confirmation with zero exceptions -- closing the compliance-gap question, though the CEO's two originally-flagged turns could not be identified after the fact well enough to confirm or rule out on their own. The mechanism was verified to have no code path that fires without producing both a recorded system-reminder and a gate-enforcement marker file.
root_cause: Investigated but not fully resolved to the level of the two specific flagged turns. Two candidate explanations were weighed: (a) the hook simply never fired on those turns because their prompts scored at or above the 3/5 quality threshold, so no confirmation was structurally owed; (b) the hook fired and the confirmation step was skipped, a genuine compliance gap. Direct transcript forensics found zero evidence anywhere in the session supporting (b) -- every genuine firing was followed by confirmation -- which is consistent with (a), though it does not itself forensically prove (a) for the two specific unidentified turns. Root cause is recorded as resolved for the general compliance-gap question, not as a forensic identification of those two turns.
remediation: No code or process change was required -- the gate's implementation was found to function exactly as documented in CLAUDE.md section 11, both in the deterministic scoring logic and in the PreToolUse enforcement mechanism. The standing takeaway, already true before this investigation but reconfirmed by it: any [PROMPT OPTIMIZER - H-P01] system-reminder is treated as an immediate, blocking instruction requiring the full optimize-then-confirm flow before any other work proceeds, on every turn, with no carryover of prior approvals.
scope_of_applicability: Surface this record when a future claim arises that a workspace hook 'fired and was silently ignored' versus 'never fired because its trigger condition genuinely was not met' -- the diagnostic method demonstrated here (scan the raw session transcript's own hook-firing attachment records, cross-reference each one against the very next tool call) generalizes to verifying any PreToolUse/UserPromptSubmit hook's real compliance history, not just H-P01.
